filter_index.video_title: Har Ki Dun - A Himalayan Tapestry Of Culture And Mountains | Visual Film By Ninad L | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: March (patches of snow from Kalkatiyadhar to Devsu Thatch)
End of October or November (first snowfall possible)
Post-November heavy snow blocks trail beyond Kalkathiyadhar
Monsoon shuts down Govind Pashu Vihar Sanctuary for about 3 months
temperature_notes: Spring/Summer (March to June): Day 18-24°C, Night 3-8°C, 2 warm layers
Autumn/Winter (September to November): Day 8-10°C, Night 0 to -5°C, 5 warm layers

terrain_challenges: Trail follows the Thamsa River with streams and rivers throughout
Wooden or cement bridges sometimes washed away, requiring rope and pulley crossings
Steep ascent to Kalkatiyadhar from Gangaad
Ledge walking and multiple boulder sections on the Kalkatiyadhar stretch
Daily ascents and descents across all five trekking days
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek starts at 6,900 ft and climbs to 11,600 ft
Reaches 10,032 ft on the second day at Kalkathiyadhar
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) above 10,000 ft
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike; even fit people climbing too high too soon are at equal risk
Winter (December) snow can appear as soon as the trailhead, sometimes even from Taluka

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month

experience_themes: Patches of snow in March from Kalkatiyadhar to Devsu Thatch
Abundance of flowers blooming in woods and meadows in April and May
Greener valley with frequent showers in late May and late June
Lush green valley in September after monsoons with clearer air
Greens turning brown then golden in October and November
First snowfall usually in mid-December with snowmen and snowball fights
primary_watchouts: Monsoon closes the trek for about 3 months
Heavy snow post-November blocks trail beyond Kalkathiyadhar making trek virtually impossible
Forest department shuts trek post-December until mid-March due to animal migration in Govind Pashu Vihar National Park and Wildlife Sanctuary
Indiahikes winds up trek operations by end of November
Trekkers during deep winter (December-February) when trail is blocked by heavy snow and forest department closure
best_for: Flower and meadow lovers (April and May)
First snowfall and snow play enthusiasts (mid-December)
not_ideal_for: Trekkers during deep winter (December-February) when trail is blocked by heavy snow and forest department closure
